lines.text: Welcome back. It's 23:00 Madison. We're {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
I'm Sister Marigold. You're listening to the Wellness Block on PRGE.
Before we begin, I want you to notice something. [pause] The sound of your own breathing.
You've been holding it. Most of you. Shallow. Tight in the chest.
That's okay. That's normal. That's why I'm here.
Let's start simple. Breathe in. [pause] All the way in. [pause] Hold for four.
One. Two. Three. Four.
Now release. Slow. Let it go.
Again. Breathe in. [pause] The air is cold tonight. You can taste it if you open a window. Don't open a window.
Hold. [pause] One. Two. Three. Four.
Release.
I can hear helicopters from here. [pause] Can you hear them where you are?
They've been circling since 22:30. Nobody at the station will say whose they are.
Government, probably. Or people with enough money to hire government's equipment.
It doesn't matter. They can't see into your home. [pause] That's a fact. Hold onto it.
Breathe in. [pause] The ones outside are not your responsibility.
That's what the Order teaches. The first rule. Not your responsibility.
Hold for four. One. Two. Three. Four.
Release.
Now I want you to do a body scan. Start at the top of your head.
Notice any tension. The forehead. The jaw. Most of you are clenching.
Relax it. You don't need to hold your face like that anymore.
Down through the neck. The shoulders. [pause] They're up. Bring them down.
Feel the weight of them. Feel how heavy they are when you let them go.
The chest. [pause] Still tight. That's where the fear lives. We all know that.
Breathe into it. Don't fight it. Just breathe. [pause] In. [pause] Four count. [pause] Out.
The belly. The hips. The thighs. [pause] Are you sitting? Standing? Lying down?
Wherever you are, you chose it. You chose to be inside. [pause] That was a choice.
Remember that in about four hours when you're tired and the mind gets loud.
You chose this. [pause] You are choosing this.
Down through the legs. The feet. [pause] All the way to the floor.
You are grounded. You are heavy. You are still.
Now bring your attention back to the breath. In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
I want to talk about forgiveness for a moment. [pause] Not of the people outside.
Of yourself. For whatever you did before 19:00 today. Whatever you said. Whatever you didn't say.
Picture that moment. [pause] Hold it gently. [pause] Now picture letting it go.
Picture a door. [pause] You're handing it through. [pause] You're closing the door.
It's not your responsibility anymore. [pause] None of it is.
ka-moretheshavim-rah. [pause] Breathe.
The helicopters are moving east now. I can feel them moving. The sound changes when they turn.
Some of you are in buildings with thick walls. Some of you live in older homes. [pause] The noise carries.
It's 23:00 now. You have eight hours until the light comes back.
Eight hours is a long time if you're afraid. [pause] And you are afraid. That's okay.
Fear is just information. [pause] It's telling you that you're alive. [pause] That you're aware.
Close your eyes if you want to. [pause] Or keep them open. [pause] There's no wrong way to do this.
Breathe in. [pause] The Order teaches that silence is where we meet ourselves. [pause] Not other people. [pause] Ourselves.
In the silence, there's no judgment. There's no news. There's no kill count. [pause] There's just you.
Four count. [pause] One. Two. Three. Four.
Out. [pause] Slow. [pause] As slow as you can make it.
You will make it to 7:00 in the morning. [pause] Most of you will. [pause] And when you do, the light will come back.
Until then, you are safe in this room. [pause] You are safe in your choice. [pause] Breathe.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 61 kills | Hour 4 delta: +23 | East Side spiking hard
Capitol Square perimeter breach reported at 21:40 — security holding inner ring | 6 confirmed deceased in clearing action
Helicopter activity over East Side and Beltline corridor all night — source unconfirmed (gov/private security speculation)
"You are choosing this." — Sister Marigold, 23:00 wellness block
